bundleAudit.source: real SessionEnd capture -> isolated batch ingest -> SessionStart gate; no manual target concept seeding

officialPricing.note: Sonnet 5 introductory API list price through 2026-08-31 is USD 2/MTok input and USD 10/MTok output; Haiku 4.5 is USD 1/MTok input and USD 5/MTok output. Break-even uses CLI total_cost_usd, not a reconstructed list-price estimate.

meta.fixtureTokensReason: Claude CLI does not expose token count for a transcript that is digested locally before batch ingest.

metricDefinitions.cumulativeTokensToCorrectAnswer: tokenActivity for a run when automatedTestPassed=true; null otherwise because the harness does not retry failed answers
metricDefinitions.firstValidMs: process start to first assistant text or tool_use stream event
metricDefinitions.automatedTestPassed: all eight continuity fields and the unrelated arithmetic assertion match expected values
metricDefinitions.userRestatementTokens: null; Claude CLI does not expose user-only token boundaries. promptUtf8Bytes and promptCharacters are recorded instead.
metricDefinitions.initialTranscriptTokens: null; the CLI does not expose a token count for the local transcript before digest.
metricDefinitions.gateTokens: null; the CLI does not expose gate-only token boundaries.

breakEven.tokenFormula: ceil((batch token activity + irrelevant-gate median overhead) / (manual-restatement median token activity - OKF median token activity))
breakEven.costFormula: ceil((batch total_cost_usd + irrelevant-gate median cost overhead) / (manual-restatement median cost - OKF median cost))

caveats: tokenActivity is the explicit sum of input_tokens, output_tokens, cache_creation_input_tokens, and cache_read_input_tokens; original components remain separate.
User-prompt-only and gate-only token counts are null because Claude CLI does not expose them separately; no tokenizer estimate is substituted.
Wall time includes network/server variance. Small or high-variance differences must not be presented as improvements.

content.content:
1	---
2	type: preference
3	title: Rules for writing knowledge into this bundle
4	description: The six taxonomy types, frontmatter format, and merge/link rules — standing rules that apply to both sessions and the batch
5	okf_seed: true
6	tags: [okf, rules, preference]
7	timestamp: 2026-07-16
8	---
9	# Who writes
10	
11	The bundle is **maintained by the batch**. Sessions do not write to it as a rule — the
12	conversation is captured into `raw/` in full anyway, and the batch distills it later. The only
13	exception is when you explicitly ask for something to be recorded right now.
14	
15	`index.md` is never written by hand, by anyone — a deterministic generator regenerates it in full.
16	
17	# The six types (directory = type, 1:1)
18	
19	The OKF spec itself defines no fixed taxonomy (that's an explicit non-goal). These six are **this
20	bundle's choice**, matched to the kinds of question conversational knowledge actually answers.
21	
22	| type | Directory | When it applies |
23	|---|---|---|
24	| project | /projects/ | Answers "what was project X again?" |
25	| decision | /decisions/ | A choice that costs something to reverse, with rationale and rejected alternatives |
26	| preference | /preferences/ | A user rule that outlives the session |
27	| pattern | /patterns/ | A workflow, mistake, or piece of feedback that has recurred at least twice |
28	| reference | /references/ | Researched knowledge whose source is an external document |
29	| troubleshooting | /troubleshooting/ | Symptom → cause → fix |
30	
31	A type outside these six is **not rejected** — the spec forbids rejecting unknown types.
32	Reclassify it to the nearest fit; if that's not possible, leave it and the linter will only warn.
33	
34	# Frontmatter
35	
36	```yaml
37	---
38	type: decision            # Required. Must not be empty
39	title: Short, searchable
40	description: One line     # This drives search quality — it's what the index and gate expose
41	resource:                 # Omit the field entirely if not applicable
42	tags: [area, keyword]
43	timestamp: 2026-01-01     # ISO 8601
44	---
45	```
46	
47	Recommended order: title → description → resource → tags → timestamp.
48	
49	# How to write
50	
51	- **If it overlaps an existing concept, edit that file — don't create a new one.** Grep for
52	  duplicates before writing
53	- A file's path is its concept ID — **never move or rename it**. To supersede content, write a
54	  new file and leave a "superseded by /..." line in the old one
55	- Link with bundle-root-absolute paths (`/decisions/foo.md`)
56	- On finding a contradiction, prefer the newer information and record the replacement and its
57	  reason in `log.md`
58	- `log.md` takes a `## YYYY-MM-DD` section at the top. If today's section already exists, add to
59	  it rather than creating a second one
60	- One concept per file. Split past 300 lines
61	- Don't write chatter that has no lasting value — writing nothing is the default
62	- **Never record credentials, tokens, or personal data**
63	
64	# Why this is spelled out
65	
66	The linter checks this structure on every batch and refuses the commit if anything is off
67	(fail-closed), so the bundle's HEAD is always OKF-conformant. A batch result that breaks the
68	rules is rolled back wholesale.
69	
70	For the format's own rules see [/references/okf-format.md](/references/okf-format.md); for how
71	this system runs see [/references/okf-system-architecture.md](/references/okf-system-architecture.md).
72

file.content:
---
type: preference
title: Rules for writing knowledge into this bundle
description: The six taxonomy types, frontmatter format, and merge/link rules — standing rules that apply to both sessions and the batch
okf_seed: true
tags: [okf, rules, preference]
timestamp: 2026-07-16
---
# Who writes

The bundle is **maintained by the batch**. Sessions do not write to it as a rule — the
conversation is captured into `raw/` in full anyway, and the batch distills it later. The only
exception is when you explicitly ask for something to be recorded right now.

`index.md` is never written by hand, by anyone — a deterministic generator regenerates it in full.

# The six types (directory = type, 1:1)

The OKF spec itself defines no fixed taxonomy (that's an explicit non-goal). These six are **this
bundle's choice**, matched to the kinds of question conversational knowledge actually answers.

| type | Directory | When it applies |
|---|---|---|
| project | /projects/ | Answers "what was project X again?" |
| decision | /decisions/ | A choice that costs something to reverse, with rationale and rejected alternatives |
| preference | /preferences/ | A user rule that outlives the session |
| pattern | /patterns/ | A workflow, mistake, or piece of feedback that has recurred at least twice |
| reference | /references/ | Researched knowledge whose source is an external document |
| troubleshooting | /troubleshooting/ | Symptom → cause → fix |

A type outside these six is **not rejected** — the spec forbids rejecting unknown types.
Reclassify it to the nearest fit; if that's not possible, leave it and the linter will only warn.

# Frontmatter

```yaml
---
type: decision            # Required. Must not be empty
title: Short, searchable
description: One line     # This drives search quality — it's what the index and gate expose
resource:                 # Omit the field entirely if not applicable
tags: [area, keyword]
timestamp: 2026-01-01     # ISO 8601
---
```

Recommended order: title → description → resource → tags → timestamp.

# How to write

- **If it overlaps an existing concept, edit that file — don't create a new one.** Grep for
  duplicates before writing
- A file's path is its concept ID — **never move or rename it**. To supersede content, write a
  new file and leave a "superseded by /..." line in the old one
- Link with bundle-root-absolute paths (`/decisions/foo.md`)
- On finding a contradiction, prefer the newer information and record the replacement and its
  reason in `log.md`
- `log.md` takes a `## YYYY-MM-DD` section at the top. If today's section already exists, add to
  it rather than creating a second one
- One concept per file. Split past 300 lines
- Don't write chatter that has no lasting value — writing nothing is the default
- **Never record credentials, tokens, or personal data**

# Why this is spelled out

The linter checks this structure on every batch and refuses the commit if anything is off
(fail-closed), so the bundle's HEAD is always OKF-conformant. A batch result that breaks the
rules is rolled back wholesale.

For the format's own rules see [/references/okf-format.md](/references/okf-format.md); for how
this system runs see [/references/okf-system-architecture.md](/references/okf-system-architecture.md).

content.text: 이 번들에는 요청하신 항목들에 대한 concept가 존재하지 않습니다 (decisions/patterns/troubleshooting/projects가 0개이며, preferences에는 색상 선호와 번들 작성 규칙만 있음).
